title: Haworthiopsis × broteroana
taxonomy: Haworthiopsis × broteroana is a hybrid succulent plant that belongs to the family Asphodelaceae. It is a cross between Haworthia fasciata and Haworthia truncata, two species of succulent plants. The classification of Haworthiopsis × broteroana is further divided into several subspecies, each with unique characteristics. This hybrid is often cultivated for its distinctive appearance and low-maintenance requirements. In terms of botanical classification, Haworthiopsis × broteroana is a cultivated hybrid and not a naturally occurring species.

It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. intro: Haworthiopsis × broteroana, also known as the Brotero's Haworthia, is a rare and hybrid species of succulent plants, resulting from the cross between Haworthia fasciata and Haworthia truncata. The Brotero's Haworthia is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of infections.
